en: "Observe the month of Abib and keep the Passover to the LORD your God, for in the month of Abib the LORD your God brought you out of Egypt by night.
zh: “你要守亞筆月，向耶和華你的 神守逾越節，因為耶和華你的 神在亞筆月的一個晚上，把你從埃及地領出來。

en: And you shall offer the Passover sacrifice to the LORD your God, from the flock or the herd, at the place that the LORD will choose, to make his name dwell there.
zh: 你要在耶和華選擇立為他名的居所的地方，把羊和牛作逾越節的祭牲獻給耶和華你的 神。

en: You shall eat no leavened bread with it. Seven days you shall eat it with unleavened bread, the bread of affliction- for you came out of the land of Egypt in haste- that all the days of your life you may remember the day when you came out of the land of Egypt.
zh: 你吃這祭的時候，不可和有酵的餅一同吃；七日之內，你要吃無酵餅，就是困苦餅，因為你是急急忙忙從埃及地出來的，好使你一生的年日都可以記念你從埃及地出來的日子。

en: No leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory for seven days, nor shall any of the flesh that you sacrifice on the evening of the first day remain all night until morning.
zh: 七天之內，在你的全境不可見有酵。第一天晚上你獻的祭肉，一點也不可剩下，留到早晨。

en: You may not offer the Passover sacrifice within any of your towns that the LORD your God is giving you,
zh: 你不能在耶和華你的 神賜給你的任何一座城裡，宰殺逾越節的祭牲；

en: but at the place that the LORD your God will choose, to make his name dwell in it, there you shall offer the Passover sacrifice, in the evening at sunset, at the time you came out of Egypt.
zh: 只能在耶和華你的 神選擇作他名的居所的地方，晚上日落的時候，就是你出埃及的時刻，宰殺逾越節的祭牲；

en: And you shall cook it and eat it at the place that the LORD your God will choose. And in the morning you shall turn and go to your tents.
zh: 你要在耶和華你的 神選擇的地方，把祭肉煮了吃；到了早晨，你才可以回到家裡去。

en: For six days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a solemn assembly to the LORD your God. You shall do no work on it.
zh: 六日之內你要吃無酵餅；到了第七日，要向耶和華你的 神守聖會；甚麼工都不可作。

en: "You shall count seven weeks. Begin to count the seven weeks from the time the sickle is first put to the standing grain.
zh: “你要計算七個七日，從開鐮收割禾稼的時候算起，共計七個七日。

en: Then you shall keep the Feast of Weeks to the LORD your God with the tribute of a freewill offering from your hand, which you shall give as the LORD your God blesses you.
zh: 你要向耶和華你的 神舉行七七節，照著耶和華你的 神賜福你的，盡你所能的獻上你手裡的甘心祭。

en: And you shall rejoice before the LORD your God, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite who is within your towns, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow who are among you, at the place that the LORD your God will choose, to make his name dwell there.
zh: 你和你的兒女、僕婢，以及在你城裡的利未人、在你們中間的寄居者、孤兒和寡婦，都要在耶和華你的 神選擇作他名的居所的地方歡樂。

en: You shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt; and you shall be careful to observe these statutes.
zh: 你也要記得你在埃及作過奴僕；你要謹守遵行這些律例。

en: "You shall keep the Feast of Booths seven days, when you have gathered in the produce from your threshing floor and your winepress.
zh: “你從禾場上和榨酒池裡收藏了出產以後，就要舉行住棚節七日。

en: You shall rejoice in your feast, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow who are within your towns.
zh: 在這節期以內，你和你的兒女、僕婢，以及住在你城裡的利未人、寄居者、孤兒和寡婦，都要歡樂。

en: For seven days you shall keep the feast to the LORD your God at the place that the LORD will choose, because the LORD your God will bless you in all your produce and in all the work of your hands, so that you will be altogether joyful.
zh: 你要在耶和華選擇的地方，向耶和華你的 神守節七日，因為耶和華你的 神，要在你一切土產上，和你手所作的一切事上，賜福給你，你就滿有歡樂。

en: "Three times a year all your males shall appear before the LORD your God at the place that he will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread, at the Feast of Weeks, and at the Feast of Booths. They shall not appear before the LORD empty-handed.
zh: 每年三次，就是在除酵節、七七節、住棚節，你所有的男丁都要在耶和華選擇的地方，朝見耶和華你的 神；但不可空手朝見耶和華。

en: Every man shall give as he is able, according to the blessing of the LORD your God that he has given you.
zh: 各人要按手中的力量，照著耶和華你的 神賜你的福，奉獻禮物。

en: "You shall appoint judges and officers in all your towns that the LORD your God is giving you, according to your tribes, and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.
zh: “在耶和華你的 神賜給你的各城裡，你要按著各支派設立審判官和官長；他們要按著公義的判斷審判人民。

en: You shall not pervert justice. You shall not show partiality, and you shall not accept a bribe, for a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and subverts the cause of the righteous.
zh: 不可屈枉正直，不可徇人的情面，不可收受賄賂，因為賄賂能使智慧人的眼變瞎，也能使義人的話顛倒過來。

en: Justice, and only justice, you shall follow, that you may live and inherit the land that the LORD your God is giving you.
zh: 你要追求公正公義，好使你能存活，並且能承受耶和華你的 神賜給你的地作產業。

en: "You shall not plant any tree as an Asherah beside the altar of the LORD your God that you shall make.
zh: “在你為耶和華你的 神築的祭壇旁邊，不可栽種甚麼樹木，作亞舍拉。

en: And you shall not set up a pillar, which the LORD your God hates.
zh: 也不可為自己豎立神柱，這是耶和華你的 神所恨惡的。”
